text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any developer. The most important aspect of clean code is that it is readable. Use clear naming conventions for variables and functions so that others can understand your intentions without comments. Each function should have a single, well-defined purpose; this makes your code easier to test and update. Remember that you write code for humans to read, not just for machines. By keeping your code simple and logical, you improve its maintainability. Future developers—and even your future self—will thank you for making the code easy to understand and modify.
